item.title: Understanding Text Representation: From Encoding to Embeddings
item.description:
🔹 Overview

In Natural Language Processing (NLP), converting text into numbers (vectors) has evolved from simple counting methods to deep semantic understanding.

This shift allows machines to move beyond keyword matching to actually understand meaning and context.

🔹 1. One-Hot Encoding (OHE)

One-Hot Encoding is the most basic form of text representation. It focuses on word presence.

Logic:

  • Create a vocabulary of unique words
  • Each word → vector with one 1 and rest 0

Example: “AI” → [0, 1, 0, 0]

item.title: Modern AI Development: From Setup to Agentic Systems
item.description:
🔹 Overview

This blog explains the modern AI stack—from environment setup using uv to building intelligent agentic systems.

🔹 Environment Setup (uv)

I used uv as a fast and lightweight alternative to traditional tools like conda.

Key commands:

  • Initialize project → uv init
  • Install dependencies → uv sync
  • Add package → uv add numpy
  • Run script → uv run main.py

🔹 AI Basics

  • AI → Simulates human intelligence
  • ML → Learns patterns from data
  • DL → Uses neural networks
  • GenAI → Generates new content (text, images, code)

🔹 Types of AI

  • Discriminative AI → Classification (predicts output)
  • Generative AI → Creates new data
  • Agentic AI → Takes actions and makes decisions

🔹 Transformers Revolution

Key milestones:

item.title: E-commerce AI Product Assistant
item.description:
🔹 Description

An AI-powered assistant that helps users find and recommend products.

🔹 Technologies Used

  • Python
  • LangChain
  • RAG
  • Groq

🔹 Features

  • Product recommendation chatbot
  • Semantic search
  • Fast LLM responses

🔹 GitHub

https://github.com/yourusername/project-link

item.title: Understanding RAG Architecture
item.description:
🔹 What is RAG?

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines:

  • Retrieval of documents
  • LLM-based answer generation

🔹 Why use RAG?

  • Improves accuracy
  • Reduces hallucination
  • Uses real data

🔹 Simple Flow

User Question → Retrieve Docs → LLM → Answer

🔹 Conclusion

RAG is a powerful technique for building reliable AI systems.

item.title: How I Built an AI Chatbot
item.description:
🔹 Overview

I built an AI chatbot using LangChain and Groq.

🔹 Steps

  1. Load documents
  2. Create embeddings
  3. Store in vector DB
  4. Build RAG pipeline

🔹 Outcome

  • Fast responses
  • Context-aware answers

🔹 Learning

Understanding RAG pipelines is key for AI applications.

item.title: RAG-Based Chatbot
item.description:
🔹 Description

A chatbot that answers questions using custom documents.

🔹 Technologies Used

  • LangChain
  • HuggingFace Embeddings
  • Vector Database

🔹 Features

  • Context-aware answers
  • Document retrieval
  • Accurate responses

🔹 GitHub

https://github.com/yourusername/project-link

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hi, I’m Krishna Raj 👋

I am an AI Engineer focused on building intelligent systems using:

  • Python
  • LangChain
  • RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation)
  • AI Agents
  • SQL & Data Engineering

I enjoy solving real-world problems using AI and building scalable applications.

🚀 What I Do

  • Build AI-powered applications
  • Develop chatbots using LLMs
  • Work on data pipelines and analytics
  • Explore LLMOps and evaluation frameworks

🎯 Goal

To become a highly skilled AI Engineer and contribute to impactful AI products.
